name: Classroom Short-answer Training Data Requires Label Audit Before Development Use
statement: Bexte and Zesch show that even in a classroom short-answer content-scoring development context with no direct learner consequence, prompt-requested labels for generated answers require audit or manual relabeling before the synthetic data can responsibly support supervised scorer training.
population_or_context: Short-answer supervised content scoring on the Student Response Analysis dataset.
time_horizon: Full text retrieved and extracted on 2026-06-02.
quantitative_note: The staged acceptable-uses finding records macro-F1 and label-conformance evidence; this finding emphasizes the stakes-sensitive label-provenance boundary.
caveats: Does not show that generated target labels can be accepted without audit.
Manual relabeling reduces the claim that the workflow is label-free.
evaluation_method: Label-conformance analysis, macro-averaged F1 on original student answers, and comparison between as-generated and manually cleaned labels.
limitations: Does not address summative or high-stakes operational scoring.

source_locator.quote_or_paraphrase_note: The finding paraphrases the generated-label setup, audit/relabeling step, and development-use boundary.
